subject: Mathematics
title: Yitang Zhang: The Unknown Who Stunned the World
description: A quick win: understand Yitang Zhang: The Unknown Who Stunned the World.

hook.text: A 58-year-old part-time lecturer with no famous results submitted a paper that rocked number theory.

buildup.text: Yitang Zhang had spent years working at a sandwich shop and as a motel accountant after his PhD.

discovery.text: He proved that infinitely many pairs of primes differ by at most 70 million—a bounded prime gap.

twist.text: Within a year, a collaborative project shrank his bound from 70 million down to 246.

climax.text: His story proved that great math can come from anyone, anywhere, at any age.

punchline.text: From sandwich shop to mathematical immortality.

quiz.question: What did Yitang Zhang prove about prime numbers?
options: That infinitely many prime pairs have a bounded gap
That there are finitely many primes
